text.title: Fine-tune vs prompt economic model — worked example
text.summary: Worked model for a support-classifier workload at 50k req/day.

list.item: training cost 800; ops overhead 400/mo; vendor risk premium 15%.

section.title: Base scenario maths
section.note: Numbers rounded; real kernel produces fractional values to 2 dp.

list.item: volume_minus_50: prompt 585, ft 290 + 400 + 44 = 734; delta = -149 (FT worse). Break-even = "never".
volume_plus_50: prompt 1755, ft 879; delta = 876. Break-even = month 1.
prompt_cost_minus_30_eoy: prompt drops to 819 (-30%); ft same 579; delta = 240. Break-even = month 4.

section.title: Recommendation
section.p: Base + volume_plus_50 + prompt_cost_minus_30_eoy all favor FT (break-even ≤ 4). volume_minus_50 makes FT a loss. Recommendation: `fine-tune` with `revisit_trigger: monthly volume below 25k req/day`.
